name: Jerry Hey
abstract: Jerry Hey (born 1950) is an American trumpeter, flugelhornist,  arranger (horns and strings), orchestrator and session musician who has played on hundreds of commercial recordings, including Michael Jackson's Thriller, "Rock with You", "Don’t Stop ‘Til You Get Enough", "Workin’ Day and Night" and the flugelhorn solo on Dan Fogelberg's hi…

interviews.title: Jerry Hey interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 2, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Findley
about_quotes.evidence: Jerry was a wonderful trumpet player... he just became a writer... Jerry would always, he would play everything he wrote before he brought it to a session... we were very busy running from one date to another date... Gary Grant was also involved with that sessions, in the trumpet section, the three
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Findley interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 6, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Bergeron
about_quotes.evidence: I kept seeing these three names: Jerry Hey, Chuck Findley, and Gary Grant. I mean on every record that I liked... I go man who are these?
about_quotes.doc_title: Wayne Bergeron interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 29, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Howie Shear
about_quotes.evidence: Chuck Findley, Jerry Hey, Gary Grant. They did a lot of the Michael Jackson stuff and the Quincy Jones stuff with Michael Jackson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howie Shear interviewed by Monk Rowe, Dunkirk, New York, October 11, 2014
